era_context: Early Bronze Age III. Egypt is in the First Intermediate Period (c. 2181–2055 BCE), a time of political fragmentation and reduced central authority. Mesopotamia remains divided among city-states and Gutian-controlled regions. The Indus Valley Civilisation continues its mature phase. Minoan Crete is in the Early Minoan II period. In China, the Xia Dynasty is traditionally dated to this era.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Pacific and most of the Americas
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: No specific events are attested for this year. The historical record for this period is fragmentary and does not support year-by-year chronology. Surviving sources (administrative texts, inscriptions, archaeological layers) provide only broad temporal frameworks, not precise annual events.
